annotations.label: Chinese remainder theorem (remainders form)

anchor.snippet: states that if one knows the remainders of the Euclidean division of an integer n

annotations.note: Nat.chineseRemainderOfFinset produces a value satisfying all congruences for a finset of pairwise-coprime moduli, the natural-number form of the lead statement.

annotations.label: Sunzi example (3, 5, 7 → 105)

anchor.snippet: If one knows that the remainder of n divided by 3 is 2

annotations.note: This concrete worked numeric example is not stated as a declaration in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sunzi's original problem

anchor.snippet: There are certain things whose number is unknown

annotations.note: A historical numeric puzzle, not formalized as a Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: CRT assertion (unique integer x)

anchor.snippet: asserts that if the n i are pairwise coprime

annotations.note: Nat.chineseRemainderOfFinset gives existence and chineseRemainderOfList_modEq_unique gives uniqueness modulo the product.

annotations.label: CRT restated via congruences

anchor.snippet: This may be restated as follows in terms of congruences

annotations.note: The decl is stated directly in terms of Nat.ModEq congruences for a finset of pairwise-coprime moduli.

annotations.label: CRT as ring isomorphism

anchor.snippet: In abstract algebra , the theorem is often restated as: if the n i are pairwise coprime, the map

annotations.note: ZMod.chineseRemainder is the ring isomorphism ZMod (m*n) ≃+* ZMod m × ZMod n for two coprime moduli; the k-factor version is Ideal.quotientInfRingEquivPiQuotient.

annotations.label: CRT as Helly family of arithmetic progressions

anchor.snippet: the infinite arithmetic progressions of integers form a Helly family

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of Helly family (only Radon's theorem), so this reformulation is absent.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of the solution

anchor.snippet: Suppose that x and y are both solutions to all the congruences

annotations.note: Nat.chineseRemainder_modEq_unique proves any common solution is congruent mod n*m (two moduli); chineseRemainderOfList_modEq_unique handles the general case.

annotations.label: Existence via injective/surjective map

anchor.snippet: maps congruence classes modulo N to sequences of congruence classes modulo n i

annotations.note: The surjectivity (hence existence) of the map to the product is proven in Ideal.quotientInfToPiQuotient_surj, though Mathlib proves it via coprimality rather than the finite counting argument.

annotations.label: Existence by explicit construction

anchor.snippet: Existence may be established by an explicit construction of x

annotations.note: Nat.chineseRemainder is a constructive (computable) definition producing the explicit solution.

annotations.label: Solution for two moduli via Bézout

anchor.snippet: Bézout's identity asserts the existence of two integers

annotations.note: Nat.chineseRemainder solves the two-modulus case using the gcd/Bézout-based chineseRemainder' construction.

annotations.label: General case by induction

anchor.snippet: The two first equations have a solution

annotations.note: Nat.chineseRemainderOfList builds the general solution by recursion over the list, combining two congruences at a time.

annotations.label: Direct construction of solution

anchor.snippet: A solution of the system of congruences is

annotations.note: Mathlib constructs a solution recursively but does not formalize the explicit closed-form sum x = Σ aᵢ Nᵢ Mᵢ.

annotations.label: Systematic search method

anchor.snippet: it suffices to check successively the integers from 0 to N until finding the solution

annotations.note: This brute-force search algorithm is not formalized as a Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Search by sieving

anchor.snippet: The search of the solution may be made dramatically faster by sieving

annotations.note: The sieving search algorithm is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Computation using existence construction

anchor.snippet: the solution may be obtained by the computation of the Bézout coefficients of the moduli

annotations.note: The Bézout-coefficient construction is realized by Nat.chineseRemainder', but the specific worked computation is not a formalized statement.

annotations.label: Reformulation as linear Diophantine system

anchor.snippet: may be rewritten as a system of linear Diophantine equations

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of CRT recast as a linear Diophantine system solved via Smith/Hermite normal form.

annotations.label: CRT over principal ideal domains

anchor.snippet: These two versions of the theorem are true in this context

annotations.note: Ideal.quotientInfRingEquivPiQuotient holds for any commutative ring with pairwise-coprime ideals, which generalizes the PID case.

annotations.label: CRT for polynomials

anchor.section: Over univariate polynomial rings and Euclidean domains
anchor.snippet: The Chinese remainder theorem for polynomials is thus

annotations.note: There is no polynomial-specific CRT, but Ideal.quotientInfRingEquivPiQuotient instantiated at R = K[X] yields the polynomial Chinese remainder theorem.

annotations.label: Lagrange interpolation as special case

anchor.snippet: A special case of Chinese remainder theorem for polynomials is Lagrange interpolation

annotations.note: Lagrange.interpolate with Lagrange.eval_interpolate_at_node formalizes Lagrange interpolation directly.

annotations.label: Hermite interpolation

anchor.snippet: Hermite interpolation is an application of the Chinese remainder theorem for univariate polynomials

annotations.note: Mathlib's Hermite files cover Hermite orthogonal polynomials, not Hermite interpolation.

annotations.label: Generalization to non-coprime moduli

anchor.snippet: The system of simultaneous congruences

annotations.note: Nat.chineseRemainder' handles the two-modulus non-coprime case under the gcd-compatibility hypothesis, but the general k-modulus solvability criterion is not formalized.

annotations.label: Two congruences (non-coprime) case

anchor.snippet: To illustrate this for the case of two congruences

annotations.note: Nat.chineseRemainder' solves two congruences a ≡ b [MOD gcd n m], exactly the non-coprime two-modulus case.

annotations.label: Coprime (comaximal) ideals

anchor.snippet: Two ideals I and J are coprime if there are elements

annotations.note: Ideal.isCoprime_iff_exists characterizes coprime ideals as ∃ i ∈ I, j ∈ J, i + j = 1, matching the comaximal definition.

annotations.label: CRT for arbitrary rings

anchor.snippet: Let I 1 , ..., I k be two-sided ideals of a ring

annotations.note: Mathlib's ideal CRT requires a commutative ring, so the noncommutative two-sided-ideal version stated here is only covered in the commutative special case.

annotations.label: Intersection equals product (commutative case)

anchor.snippet: the ideal intersection of pairwise coprime ideals is equal to their product

annotations.note: Ideal.prod_eq_iInf_of_pairwise_isCoprime states the product of pairwise-coprime ideals equals their intersection (iInf).

annotations.label: Central pairwise orthogonal idempotents

anchor.snippet: are central idempotents that are pairwise orthogonal

annotations.note: CompleteOrthogonalIdempotents captures a family of idempotents that are pairwise orthogonal and sum to 1, with centrality handled via ringEquivOfIsMulCentral.

annotations.label: Idempotent equivalence form of CRT

anchor.snippet: is the equivalence between giving pairwise coprime two-sided ideals with a zero intersection

annotations.note: CompleteOrthogonalIdempotents.ringEquivOfComm gives the product decomposition from central orthogonal idempotents, but the full bidirectional equivalence with coprime zero-intersection ideals is not packaged as one theorem.

annotations.label: Decomposition of surjections of finite abelian groups

anchor.section: Decomposition of surjections of finite abelian groups
anchor.snippet: we can use the Chinese remainder theorem to give a complete description of any such map

annotations.note: This CRT-based description of surjections between finite abelian groups is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dedekind's theorem (linear independence of characters)

anchor.snippet: Then any finite family ( f i ) i ∈ I of distinct monoid homomorphisms f i : M → k is linearly independent

annotations.note: linearIndependent_monoidHom is exactly Dedekind's linear independence of distinct monoid homomorphisms into a domain.
